I
Re:
F.rrn Por-rrrcAr- PnACTrces CoMrvrrssroN
-ll8 J Street o Suite 620 o Sacrantento. CA 9581-t-l-129
(916) .il2-5660 o Fax (916) 322-0886
June 23, 2010
Alf W. Brandt
3605 Miwok Place
Davis, California 956 I 8
Your Request for Advice
Our File No. I-10-097
Dear Mr. Brandt:
QUESTTON
How are payments you receive from the American Bar Association's Section of the
Environment, Energy and Resources to cover the costs of travel and lodging expenses while
conducting your duties as a Section Committee Chair to be reported on your Statement of
Economic Interests?
CONCLUSION
Because the value of the work you perform for the American Bar Association's Section
of the Environment, Energy and Resources is presumably greater than or equal to the payments
you receive to cover the costs of travel and lodging, those payments should be reported as
income on yollr Statement of Economic Interests under the Act.
' The Political Retorm Act is contained in Government Code Sections ttl000 through 91014. All statutory
rct'erences are to the Covernment Code, unless otherwise indicated. 'Ihe regulations tlf the Fair Political Practices
Commission are contained in Sections l8l l0 through 18997 of Title 2 of the California Code of Regulations. All
regulatory ret'erences are to 'Iitle 2. Division 6 of the Calitbrnia Code of Regulations. unless otherwise indicuted.
r Intirrmal assistancedoesnotproli<Iethercqueskrrwiththeimmunityprovicledbyanrlpinionortirrmal
uritten advice. (Section 83 I l-l: Regulation ltil29(cX-l).)
This letter responds to your request for advice regarding the gift and income reporting
provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").' The Commission will not advise with
respect to past conduct. (Regulation 18329(b)(8XA).) Therefore, nothing in this letter should be
construed to evaluate any conduct that may have already taken place. Any conclusions
contained herein apply only to prospective actions. Because your questions are general in nature,
we are treating your request as one for informal assistance.2


I
File No. t-10-097
Page No. 2
FACTS
You work for the California Legislature as the Principal Consultant for the Assembly
Committee on Water, Parks & Wildlife. In 2008, the Chair of the American Bar Association,s
Section of the Environment, Energy and Resources ("Section") appointed you to chair a Section
committee. You therefore serve as part of the Section's nationwG leaderihip and as part of
your responsibilities the Section expects you to participate in the spring and titt Section Council
meetings. This is a volunteer unpaid position. However, the Section pays for your travel and
lodging costs when attending Section Council meetings because you *oik for a govemment
agency. You pay for your own meals.
At the Council meetings, you confer with the Council as to your committee's activities
and generally participate in Council deliberations, which occasionaily lead to additional
assignments. Your particular committee arranges for funding for Seition public
service/education projects related to the environment. You are seeking advice on how to
properly report the travel and lodging payments you receive from the Section on your Statement
of Economic Interests (Form 700).
ANALYSIS
Because you are a designated employee of the California Legislature you are required to
report your economic interests with respect your disclosure category in your ig"n"y's conflict-
of-interest code. (Section 87302.) This includes reporting the sources oi gifts ind income.
"Income" is broadly defined in Section 82030(a) to include all forms of payment received
by a public official, including reimbursement of expenses, per diem payments ord-giftr. Section
82028(a) defines a gift as "any payment to the extent that consideration of equal oigreater value
is not received . . -." If the value of the-work an official performs for any given entity is of equal
or greater value than payments to an official to cover the costs of travel ini toAging associated
with the work performed by the official, consideration of equal or greater value has been
provided for the payments received, and the payments are considerid to be income to the official
under the Act- An official has the burden of showing that he or she provided consideration of
equal or greater value in exchange for payment of travel and Iodging expenses in order for a
payment to be considered income as opposed to a gift under the Act. If an official does not meet
this btrrden of proof, the reimbursement will constitute a gift to the official. (Riddte Advice
Letter No. .4'-89-200 and Quan Advice Letter No. A-89-1g2.)
Because you perform significant work for the Section, and your position is a volunteer
position, it is likely that the value of the work you perform for the Section exceeds the value you
receive in travel and lodging reimbursements. Therefore, with respect to future payments foi
travel and lodging you receive from the Section while on Section business, you itrould report the


,
File No. I-10-097
Page No. 3
payments you receive from the Section as income on your Statement of Economic Interest (Form
7oo).1
If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660.
Sincerely,
Scott Hallabrin
General Counsel
-4ulzl,^ K.&u*-
By: Sukhi K. Brar
Counsel, Legal Division
SKB jgl
t Ho*ever, if you do not pertbrm w'ork for the Section that is of equal or greater value than the travel and
lodging payments you receive you will be required to report such payments as gitts on your Statement of Economic
Interests and those gitts would be subject to the $420 gifi Iimit. (Section 89503. Regulation t89+0.2)
